packageRecommendation.name: @nicia-ai/lachesis-cli
packageRecommendation.versionPolicy: Synchronize the first CLI prerelease with the next repository alpha; do not establish an independent version line yet.
packageRecommendation.publicationSet: Publish the CLI and only packages with actual source, dependency, or metadata changes. Do not republish all five alpha.3 packages solely for numeric synchronization.
packageRecommendation.importSurface: No supported JavaScript import surface and no public TypeScript declarations; the package is a binary-only product.

requiredMetadataAndDocumentationDelta: Set private to false only during a separately reviewed release-preparation change.
Set version to 0.1.0-alpha.4 only during separately authorized release preparation.
Replace the plan-inspector description with catalog manifest, comparison, conformance, and detached report-verification language.
Replace the private workspace README with a self-contained registry getting-started guide; remove repository-relative links.
Add the stable exit-code policy, POSIX and GitHub Actions examples, ESM-only/CommonJS statement, same-UID filesystem limitation, bounded-input behavior, and troubleshooting.
Add a CLI changelog covering the private-to-public transition and explicitly state that no Lachesis library API changed.
Retain exact alpha.3 dependency pins unless a dependency actually changes before release.
releaseBlockers: The packed CLI manifest is private.
The packed README is workspace-oriented and contains a broken ../../README.md link.
The package description omits catalog conformance and detached verification.
The default-parallel release test gate has a reproduced contention-sensitive five-second timeout.
audit:release-checksums compares the current authorized workflow correction with the older frozen pre-correction workflow digest and therefore cannot pass on the baseline.
nonchanges: No public library export is required.
No report schema, protocol, or exit code should change.
No provider, network, credential, or research capability is part of the CLI release.
No synchronized republication of unchanged alpha.3 packages is required.
